world.title: Valero Shuts Major Refinery After Explosion as Fuel Markets Tighten
world.description: An explosion at Valero’s Port Arthur refinery has shut down a key U.S. fuel facility, intensifying supply concerns and adding upward pressure to already rising diesel and gasoline prices.

world.title: Body discovered after second fire in arson attack house where Tadhg Farrell and Mary Holt died last year
world.description: Police are investigating "all the circumstances" of the latest blaze at a home in Edenderry, Ireland, where a four-year-old boy and his great-aunt died last year.

world.title: Nigerian lady urges African governments to use mud or clay for public buildings to reduce heat and energy costs
world.description: A Nigerian lady urges African governments to return to mud buildings, arguing that cement structures trap heat, increase energy costs, and worsen living conditions. Read more.

world.description: The evacuations already began in Ennedi Est Province over the weekend, with the government targeting to relocate at least 2,300 refugees, mostly women and children.

science.title: New tool maps the landscape of student knowledge using short quizzes
science.description: A Dartmouth study reports a technique for creating detailed landscape maps of what students know based on their performance on short multiple-choice quizzes. The researchers’ mathematical framework could be used to power a new generation of personalized AI tutors that can identify what individual students do and do not understand, then customize feedback to improve learning. The researchers released a public demo where users can construct an interactive map of their knowledge.

science.title: Carbon Contamination Found To Shape Contact Electrification In Oxides
science.description: Research shows adventitious carbon breaks symmetry in oxide contact electrification, explaining inconsistent charging in nominally identical surfaces.

science.title: We’ve Been Underestimating Flying Foxes
science.description: Australia used to incinerate the large fruit bats, but research suggests they bring hundreds of millions of dollars in benefits to the country’s economy.

science.title: Mathematical framework maps landscape of student knowledge via short quizzes
science.description: When we learn something new, that information does not exist in isolation. It integrates into the complex landscape of our knowledge, forging connections with existing ideas and opening up possibilities for new learning. In a study in Nature Communications, Dartmouth researchers report a mathematical technique for mapping the unique landscape of a student's conceptual knowledge from their performance on short multiple-choice quizzes.

science.title: Stolen chloroplasts maintained by host-made proteins offer clues to plant cell origins
science.description: Every plant cell is the product of a biological merger billions of years ago. Chloroplasts are key structures in plants and algae that capture sunlight, but originally they were free-living bacteria that took up residence inside another cell. Over time, these partners became more closely integrated by sharing genes, proteins, and roles.
